name: La Manga del Mar Menor Coastal Trail
name_original: Salinas de Marchamalo y La Manga del Mar Menor — desde el Cabo de Palos
description: Road cycling a moderate 25.3-mile route from Cabo de Palos, exploring La Manga del Mar Menor and the Marchamalo Salt Flats.
region_intro: Road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo traverse a landscape characterized by coastal paths along the Mar Menor lagoon and the Mediterranean Sea. The terrain is generally flat, featuring salt flats and low-lying coastal areas, with minimal elevation changes. Routes often provide views of the Mar Menor, Cabo de Palos Lighthouse, and the surrounding natural park. Best road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo The most popular road cycling route is La Manga del Mar Menor Coastal Trail, a 25.3 miles (40.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers extensive views along the La Manga strip. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is The Mar Menor – Cabo de Palos Lighthouse loop from Mercadona, a moderate 23.8 miles (38.2 km) path. This route circles the Mar Menor, passing the iconic Cabo de Palos Lighthouse. Local road cyclists also love the Club Náutico Mar Menor – Blick auf das Mar Menor loop from Rincón de San Ginés, a 34.6 miles (55.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling in Salinas De Marchamalo is defined by coastal paths, salt flats, and the Mar Menor lagoon. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Salinas De Marchamalo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Salinas De Marchamalo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: Salinas De Marchamalo offers over 35 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good selection of easy, moderate, and challenging options to explore the coastal landscape.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: Yes, there are 7 easy road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature flat terrain along the coast, making them very accessible.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: The best time for road cycling in Salinas De Marchamalo is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. Temperatures are milder, and the weather is typically pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: Road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo are characterized by generally flat terrain, traversing coastal paths along the Mar Menor lagoon and the Mediterranean Sea. You'll often ride through salt flats and low-lying coastal areas with minimal elevation changes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: Given the predominantly flat terrain and coastal paths, many of the easier routes in Salinas De Marchamalo are suitable for families. These paths offer safe and enjoyable cycling experiences away from heavy traffic, with scenic views of the Mar Menor.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: Many routes offer extensive views of the Mar Menor lagoon and the Mediterranean Sea. You can also spot the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1010640889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo de Palos Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and explore the surrounding natural park areas, including the salt flats.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40396738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Mar Menor – Cabo de Palos Lighthouse loop from Mercadona&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that circles the Mar Menor and passes the lighthouse.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: Route durations vary depending on distance and your pace. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010640889/la-manga-del-mar-menor-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Manga del Mar Menor Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 25.3-mile route, typically takes around 2 hours to complete. Shorter routes can be done in under an hour, while longer ones might take 3 hours or more.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning coastal views, the flat and accessible terrain, and the opportunity to explore the unique Mar Menor lagoon and its surroundings.

Q: Are there advanced road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: While the region is known for its flat coastal terrain, there are 3 routes classified as difficult for those seeking a greater challenge. These routes might involve longer distances or slightly more varied terrain, though significant elevation changes are rare.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and coastal areas near the starting points of many routes. Look for public parking facilities in places like La Manga del Mar Menor, Cabo de Palos, or Rincón de San Ginés, which serve as common access points for cyclists.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road cycling routes in Salinas De Marchamalo?
A: For a route that combines the unique salt flats with coastal views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40394192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchamalo Salt Flats – Puerto de Atamaría loop from Consorcio&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route offers a different perspective of the region's natural beauty.
tips:
Originally Cabo de Palos was a fishing village.
Currently with a lot of history behind it, it has become an ideal tourist site for various activities, both gastronomic and sports.
It is worth going to the beautiful lighthouse that offers us some beautiful photographs.

This protected space has a rich history regarding the production of salt ("white gold") that marked an era in the area of Cartagena and the Mar Menor.
At the end of the 19th century, the salt industry was established, which over the years lost momentum due to low productivity and became a mainly tourist area with a real estate boom.
Today it is a beautiful place to visit and tour where you can still appreciate some constructions from the salt era and also spot native birds.

At the end of the Manga we will find several salt mills such as the one in Veneziola or the No. 168 that were used to grind salt.

The place is very quiet and ideal for contemplating the splendid views of the sea. Nearby we can visit the Bridge of Laughter that will give us a beautiful picture.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain along this route is predominantly flat, making it suitable for a wide range of cyclists. The main routes are generally well-paved and easy to ride, though some segments may have unpaved sections. You'll be cycling on a narrow strip of land, La Manga, with views of both the Mediterranean Sea and the Mar Menor lagoon.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning and diverse scenery. The route offers picturesque views of both the vast Mediterranean Sea and the calm, shallow Mar Menor lagoon. You'll pass beautiful beaches, charming seaside towns, and natural areas like the Marchamalo Salt Flats, known for their rich biodiversity, including flamingos and other aquatic birds. The area also offers opportunities for unique sunsets.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its predominantly flat and well-paved surfaces, this route is often described as easy to ride and suitable for a wide range of cyclists, including families and those seeking a less strenuous ride. While classified as moderate, it leans towards the easier end of the spectrum.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the iconic Cabo de Palos Lighthouse, the ecologically significant Marchamalo Salt Flats, and the historic Salt Mills of Mar Menor. The unique geographical setting of La Manga itself, cycling between two seas, is also a major highlight.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The area is known for its good weather and many days of sunshine, making it attractive for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying mild conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route from Cabo de Palos?
faqs.answer: As the route starts from Cabo de Palos, you should look for parking options within the town. Cabo de Palos has various public parking areas, especially near the lighthouse or the town center, which provide convenient access to the starting point of the route.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route for a break?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are plenty of opportunities to stop at local bars and restaurants in the seaside towns along the route, particularly in Cabo de Palos and various points along La Manga del Mar Menor. These offer convenient spots for refreshments and meals during your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Transmurciana 04, Transmurciana 05, and the Sendero del Mediterráneo - Murcia, as well as several local 'Paseo Marítimo' sections.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs can vary. While the route itself is mostly on public roads and paths, specific rules for natural areas like the Marchamalo Salt Flats or beaches may apply. It's advisable to keep dogs on a leash and check local signage for any restrictions, especially in protected areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in the Paisaje Protegido Espacios Abiertos e Islas del Mar Menor?
faqs.answer: Generally, no special permits or entrance fees are required for cycling on public roads and designated paths within the Paisaje Protegido Espacios Abiertos e Islas del Mar Menor. However, as it is a protected landscape, cyclists are expected to respect environmental regulations, stay on marked routes, and avoid disturbing wildlife, particularly in sensitive areas like the Marchamalo Salt Flats.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see, especially in the Salinas de Marchamalo?
faqs.answer: The Marchamalo Salt Flats are a protected space known for their rich biodiversity. You have a good chance of spotting various aquatic birds, including flamingos, especially during migration seasons. The unique wetland ecosystem supports a diverse range of bird species.
